story.asA: a developer building services with the framework
story.iWant: configuration and context management to be interface-based
story.soThat: I can provide custom configurations and mock contexts in tests
story.tasks: - Task 1: Analyze current configuration and context implementations (AC: 1, 2) - Task 2: Create configuration and context interface definitions (AC: 1, 2, 3) - Task 3: Implement interface compliance and testing (AC: 4, 5) - Task 4: Preserve existing API contracts (AC: 4)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Interface Definition - ConfigProviderInterface and EndorContextInterface are defined in sdk/interfaces/config.go 2. Configuration Abstraction - Current configuration loading logic is abstracted behind interfaces with environment and custom source support 3. Context Operations - EndorContext operations become mockable for testing with interface-based access patterns 4. Implementation Compliance - Current configuration and context implementations satisfy their respective interfaces 5. Testing Infrastructure - Interface compliance tests verify implementations and demonstrate mocking patterns

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR5: Developers can unit test services without MongoDB dependencies through interface mocking. FR10: Framework eliminates hard-coded singleton dependencies (like MongoDB client). FR11: Developers can inject custom implementations for any framework dependency.

doc.title: Refactored Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Interface Segregation with Smart Composition - Following Go's interface philosophy of "accept interfaces, return structs". ConfigProviderInterface for configuration, granular interfaces that are easy to mock and compose as needed.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Story 1.3: Extract Configuration and Context Interfaces - Developer wants configuration and context management to be interface-based for mocking contexts in tests. ConfigProviderInterface and EndorContextInterface defined with environment-based and custom configuration sources.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Interface Foundation & Testability
doc.snippet: ConfigProviderInterface for configuration access abstraction. EndorContextInterface for request context management. Component package sdk/interfaces/config.go - Configuration and context management interfaces.

artifact.reason: Core configuration struct that needs to be abstracted behind interface
Singleton configuration access pattern that needs interface abstraction
Generic context struct with session and payload that needs interface abstraction
Session context struct that provides authentication context
Server initialization uses GetConfig() singleton - needs interface injection
Existing service interface pattern to follow for configuration interfaces
Existing repository interface pattern to follow for configuration interfaces
Interface compliance test pattern to follow for configuration interface testing

story-context.constraints: - Follow Interface Segregation with Smart Composition pattern from architecture document - Implement "accept interfaces, return structs" Go philosophy for configuration layer - Configuration interfaces must support both environment-based and custom configuration sources - Context propagation patterns used throughout framework must remain intact - Interface design should enable testing with different configuration values - Must preserve current initialization and startup patterns for backward compatibility - Follow established interface patterns from sdk/interfaces/service.go and repository.go - Add interface compliance tests to existing sdk/interfaces_test.go - Zero breaking changes to current configuration access patterns - Framework Requirements: Configuration layer enables Epic 1 testability goals

interface.name: ConfigProviderInterface
interface.signature: type ConfigProviderInterface interface { GetServerPort() string GetDocumentDBUri() string IsHybridResourcesEnabled() bool IsDynamicResourcesEnabled() bool GetDynamicResourceDocumentDBName() string Reload() error }

interface.name: EndorContextInterface
interface.signature: type EndorContextInterface[T any] interface { GetMicroServiceId() string GetSession() Session GetPayload() T SetPayload(payload T) GetResourceMetadataSchema() RootSchema GetCategoryID() *string SetCategoryID(categoryID *string) GetGinContext() *gin.Context }

tests.standards: Interface compliance tests ensure configuration implementations satisfy contracts using var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern. Unit tests demonstrate mocking patterns for configuration-dependent business logic. Context propagation tests verify interface abstractions work correctly. Configuration source tests validate environment and file-based loading. Follow testify/mock framework for mock implementations with behavior configuration capabilities.

test.desc: Interface compliance tests for ConfigProviderInterface and EndorContextInterface definitions
Unit tests demonstrating configuration interface mocking with custom test configurations
Context propagation tests verifying EndorContext operations work through interface abstractions
Integration tests validating existing configuration and context implementations satisfy interfaces
Mock implementation tests showing how to provide test configurations for different environments
